contextBlurb: Alberta's housing policy landscape as of April 2026 is shaped by the UCP government's dual focus on supply-side reform and affordability supports. Key enacted measures include Bill 20 (Municipal Affairs Statutes Amendment Act, 2024), which exempted non-profit affordable housing from property taxes and streamlined development approvals, and Bill 32 (Financial Statutes Amendment Act, 2024, No. 2), which indexed seniors lodge rates and social housing thresholds. The province continues to implement its 10-year 'Stronger Foundations' affordable housing strategy (launched 2021), targeting 25,000 additional supported households by 2031, while actively addressing encampment homelessness through a $430 million provincial investment and a joint Canada-Alberta Unsheltered Homelessness and Encampments Initiative signed in March 2025.

legislation.title: Service Alberta Statutes Amendment Act, 2024
legislation.summary: Amends the Condominium Property Act (RSA 2000 cC-22) with key sections proclaimed in force on February 15, 2026; updates governance and disclosure rules affecting condominium housing developments across Alberta.

legislation.title: Stronger Foundations: Alberta's 10-Year Affordable Housing Strategy
legislation.summary: A 10-year provincial strategy launched November 2021 targeting housing for an additional 25,000 low-income households by 2031 (a 40%+ increase); implemented through the Affordable Housing Partnership Program (launched December 2022), the Affordable Housing Needs Assessment, the Housing Supports for Survivors of Gender-Based Violence program (launched April 2024), and the Affordable Housing Asset Management Framework.

legislation.title: Residential Tenancies Act
legislation.summary: Alberta's core landlord-tenant statute, last substantively updated with AR 199/2025 (amending the Residential Tenancies Exemption Regulation) and ongoing review by the Alberta Law Reform Institute (2023–2026); currently requires landlords to pay annual interest on security deposits (effective January 1, 2024) and permits fully digital lease agreements.

legislation.title: Unsheltered Homelessness and Encampments Initiative – Alberta Agreement
legislation.summary: A bilateral federal-provincial agreement signed March 2025 providing close to $35 million in federal funding over two years for Calgary, Edmonton, Lethbridge, and Red Deer; funds Community Encampment Response Plans to move people from encampments into stable housing with wrap-around supports, complementing Alberta's $430 million provincial homelessness investment for 2024–2026.

legislation.title: Affordable Housing Partnership Program
legislation.summary: Capital funding program under Stronger Foundations providing provincial contributions of up to one-third of eligible project costs for new construction, renovation, or redevelopment of affordable and mixed-income housing; cost-matched through the Canada–Alberta Bilateral Agreement under the National Housing Strategy.

legislation.title: Alberta Housing Act
legislation.summary: The foundational statute enabling the province to provide basic housing accommodation for persons requiring financial or social assistance; most recently amended by Bill 20 (2024) to establish the affordable housing accommodation designation and tax-exemption framework, and by Bill 32 (2024) to index lodge rates under the Alberta Escalator, effective January 2025.

legislation.title: Financial Statutes Amendment Act, 2024 (No. 2)
legislation.summary: Amends the Alberta Housing Act to introduce the 'Alberta Escalator,' standardizing CPI-based indexing of seniors' minimum monthly disposable income amounts used to calculate lodge rates; also adjusts income thresholds in social housing and income-support programs effective January 1, 2025 and annually thereafter.

legislation.title: Municipal Affairs Statutes Amendment Act, 2024
legislation.summary: Exempts non-profit subsidized affordable housing from municipal and education property taxes starting the 2025 tax year; allows municipalities to create multi-year residential property tax incentives to encourage housing development; limits non-statutory studies for development approvals and restricts extra public hearings; removes inclusionary housing provisions from Calgary and Edmonton city charters and clarifies off-site levy rules to reduce barriers to housing supply.

legislation.billCode: Social Housing Accommodation Regulation (AR 244/1994, as amended)
legislation.title: Social Housing Accommodation Regulation
legislation.summary: Governs eligibility and priority-of-need scoring for Alberta's social housing programs; amended in 2023 to allow housing providers to prioritize target populations most in need, and evaluated in 2024–25 under the Stronger Foundations strategy to confirm the amendments met their objectives.

legislation.title: Alberta Action Plan on Homelessness
legislation.summary: Announced October 2022 with $63 million in additional funding over two years, this provincial plan funds emergency and transitional shelters (24/7 operations), navigation centres (including Edmonton's 103rd Avenue centre opened in early 2024), and housing-with-supports programming in Alberta's seven major cities to reduce and prevent chronic homelessness.

legislation.billCode: Municipal Affairs and Housing Statutes Amendment Act, 2026
legislation.title: Municipal Affairs and Housing Statutes Amendment Act, 2026
legislation.summary: Proposed 2026 legislation intended to improve clarity, consistency, and accountability in municipal governance as it relates to housing and municipal affairs in Alberta.

legislation.title: Residential Tenancies Amendment Act, 2023 (Rent Control)
legislation.summary: A private member's bill introduced in the 2023 session of the 31st Legislature that proposed capping annual rent increases at 2% or CPI (whichever is lower) for residential tenancies through December 31, 2025, with a landlord application process to the Dispute Resolution Service for exceptions; did not proceed to enactment.
